methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia, a Series C-stage transportation company founded by Valery Miftakhov, has raised $150M+ to date. The transportation sector peer set includes seven private companies—notably Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft—though no public comparables exist. The narrow peer cohort (two same-stage, zero adjacent-stage, zero similar-tier) is insufficient for a robust median-based valuation cross-check.

Given the limited peer universe, stage-progression methodology provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised ($150M) as a baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, the implied valuation reaches $255M. This method assumes historical funding-round patterns hold and that capital deployed reflects underlying value creation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $217M (low) to $293M (high), with a median estimate of $255M. The 35% range reflects uncertainty inherent in single-method triangulation and the absence of comparable transaction or trading data. Key uncertainties include ZeroAvia's path to revenue, the competitive positioning of hydrogen propulsion in aviation versus alternative powertrains, and capital efficiency relative to private transportation peers. Validation of this range would benefit from closer examination of burn rate, technical milestones achieved post-Series B, and customer pipeline visibility.
